lede: A look at September 23 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Bruce Springsteen and Augustus, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Teresa Lewis
events.desc: Teresa Lewis becomes the first woman to be executed by the U.S. state of Virginia since 1912, and the first woman in the state to be executed by lethal injection.

events.title: Kauhajoki school shooting
events.desc: Matti Saari kills ten people at a school in Finland before committing suicide.

events.title: Richard Nixon
events.desc: After being accused of financial improprieties, Senator Richard Nixon delivers his "Checkers speech" nationwide on television and radio, defending his actions and successfully salvaging his nomination as the Republican candidate for Vice President.

events.title: 1920 Louisiana hurricane
events.desc: The Louisiana hurricane dissipates over Kansas after forcing around 4,500 people to evacuate and causing $1.45 million in damages.

events.title: United Mine Workers of America
events.desc: The United Mine Workers of America launch a strike which eventually escalated into the Colorado Coalfield War.

events.title: Cape Virgenes
events.desc: On the night of 23–24 September, the steamship Arctique runs aground near Cape Virgenes leading to the discovery of nearby placer gold, beginning the Tierra del Fuego gold rush.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: John Paul Jones, naval commander of the United States, on board the USS Bonhomme Richard, wins the Battle of Flamborough Head.

events.title: First English Civil War
events.desc: First English Civil War: The Battle of Powick Bridge, the first engagement between the primary field armies of the Royalists and the Parliamentarians, ends in a Royalist victory.

events.title: Battle of Arnemuiden
events.desc: The Battle of Arnemuiden, in which a French force defeats the English, is the first naval battle of the Hundred Years' War and the first naval battle in which gunpowder artillery is used.

events.title: Pope Callixtus II
events.desc: Pope Callixtus II and Holy Roman Emperor Henry V agree to the Concordat of Worms to put an end to the Investiture Controversy.

deaths.text: Charles K. Kao, Hong Kong-American-British electrical engineer and physicist
Pablo Neruda, Chilean poet and diplomat, Nobel Prize laureate

observances.text: Christian feast day: Cissa of Crowland (or of Northumbria)
Christian feast day: Blessed Francisco de Paula Victor
